pattern: I'd like to inform you that ...
b1: I'd like to inform you that the office is closed today.
I'd like to inform you that your package has arrived.
I'd like to inform you that the meeting time has changed.
I'd like to inform you that we have a new team member.
I'd like to inform you that the test starts at 10 AM.
b2: I'd like to inform you that your application has been successfully processed.
I'd like to inform you that the project deadline has been extended by a week.
I'd like to inform you that we are launching a new website next month.
I'd like to inform you that there will be a scheduled power outage this weekend.
I'd like to inform you that the board of directors has approved the budget.
c1: I'd like to inform you that the merger has been finalized following extensive negotiations.
I'd like to inform you that we are implementing a new policy regarding data privacy.
I'd like to inform you that a significant shift in market trends has been observed.
I'd like to inform you that the legal department has raised concerns about the contract.
I'd like to inform you that we have initiated a comprehensive review of our operations.

b1: The key takeaway here is to be on time.
The key takeaway here is that we need to save money.
The key takeaway here is to practice every day.

b1: To clarify, I mean we should meet at the station.
To clarify, I mean the red book, not the blue one.
To clarify, I mean I will finish it by tomorrow.
To clarify, I mean it's free for students.
To clarify, I mean we are going by bus.

To clarify, I mean we should prioritize quality over quantity.
c1: To clarify, I mean the strategic realignment will necessitate some structural changes.
To clarify, I mean our current methodology may be insufficient for this scale of data.
To clarify, I mean the environmental impact must be assessed before we proceed.
To clarify, I mean that the correlation found does not necessarily imply causation.
To clarify, I mean we must reconcile our professional goals with our ethical standards.

b1: According to the report, it will rain tomorrow.

According to the report, more people are visiting the park.

According to the report, there is a clear discrepancy between the two data sets.
According to the report, systemic biases continue to hinder organizational growth.

b1: Let me rephrase that: the shop is closed.
Let me rephrase that: we are too late.
Let me rephrase that: I don't like this idea.
Let me rephrase that: it's too expensive.
Let me rephrase that: we should wait here.
b2: Let me rephrase that: we need to find a more efficient way of working.
Let me rephrase that: the current plan is not meeting our expectations.
Let me rephrase that: we are looking for someone with more experience.

Let me rephrase that: we must prioritize long-term stability over short-term gains.

pattern: What I meant to say was ...
b1: What I meant to say was I am sorry.
What I meant to say was thank you for the help.
What I meant to say was let's go later.
What I meant to say was it's very cold.
What I meant to say was the movie was okay.
b2: What I meant to say was that we should reconsider the entire proposal.
What I meant to say was I didn't mean to offend anyone with my comment.
What I meant to say was we need to be more careful with our spending.
What I meant to say was that the results were better than we expected.
What I meant to say was that I'm not sure if this is the right time.
c1: What I meant to say was that the ethical implications were not fully considered.
What I meant to say was the complexity of the issue transcends simple solutions.
What I meant to say was our focus should be on the qualitative aspects of the study.
What I meant to say was that the systemic barriers are deeply ingrained in the culture.
What I meant to say was we need to align our strategy with the evolving market landscape.

pattern: Just to make sure we're on the same page, ...
translation: 우리가 같은 생각을 하고 있는지 확인하기 위해, ...
b1: Just to make sure we're on the same page, the party is at 7 PM.
Just to make sure we're on the same page, I'll bring the pizza.
Just to make sure we're on the same page, we are meeting at the park.
Just to make sure we're on the same page, the homework is due tomorrow.
Just to make sure we're on the same page, you are paying for the movie.
b2: Just to make sure we're on the same page, the main goal is to increase sales.
Just to make sure we're on the same page, everyone needs to attend the workshop.
Just to make sure we're on the same page, the budget for this project is fixed.
Just to make sure we're on the same page, we are not changing the design yet.
Just to make sure we're on the same page, your role is to manage the social media.
c1: Just to make sure we're on the same page, the primary objective is long-term sustainability.
Just to make sure we're on the same page, we are adopting a more cautious approach.
Just to make sure we're on the same page, the legal team must sign off on everything.
Just to make sure we're on the same page, the new policy applies to all global branches.
Just to make sure we're on the same page, our priority is resolving the ethical dilemma.

This confirms that the organizational restructuring has improved overall efficiency.
This confirms that our hypothesis regarding consumer behavior was correct.

pattern: The important point here is not ..., but ...
b1: The important point here is not the price, but the quality.
The important point here is not the time, but the effort.
The important point here is not the answer, but the process.
The important point here is not the score, but the game.
The important point here is not the color, but the size.
b2: The important point here is not who made the mistake, but how we fix it.
The important point here is not the quantity of work, but the value it brings.
The important point here is not the technology itself, but how we use it.
The important point here is not the destination, but what we learn on the journey.
The important point here is not the past, but what we do in the future.
c1: The important point here is not the individual achievement, but the collective impact.
The important point here is not the superficial data, but the underlying trends.
The important point here is not the legislative text, but its practical application.
The important point here is not the short-term profit, but the long-term sustainability.
The important point here is not the theoretical framework, but the empirical evidence.
